metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_423
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig. 1: Cntnap4 is present at presynaptic fraction, Fc-tagged Cntnap4 binds to soma and proximal dendrites, apposed to gephyrin puncta.

13/4/2017 Pim
- Cntnap4 has a TM domain. The Fc-tagged Cntnap4 construct is the extracellular domain of this protein. Fig.1 suggests that Cntnap4 is a presynaptic TM protein that interacts with proteins attached to gephyrin-positive postsynaptic elements. For this to occur the protein must be positioned in the presynaptic plasma membrane. This is not directly shown, but implied in the paper.
- "Integral component ..." is inferred form the TM domain of Cntnap4.. Experimental description: Biochemical fractionation was performed using mouse brain, the in vitro staining with Fc-tagged Cntnap4 extracellular domain was done on rat neurons, cDNA species for Fc-tagged Cntnap4 extracellular domain was not specified. These experiments are featured in Fig. 1.

Litteral from M&M
"Presynaptic and postsynaptic fractionation. Synaptosomal fractions from mouse
brain were prepared as described previously"
"Hippocampal cultures. Cultures were prepared from embryonic day 19 Sprague
Dawley ratembryonic brain tissue."

13/4/2017 Pim:
- data in Fig.1D on rat material is sufficient to claim the selected term "presynapse". Therefore mouse is the correctly annotated protein. The recombinant Cntnap4-Fc protein is used to justify the AE to GABAergic synapse.
